title: Batch Platform: batch_processing Feature Flag Required
description: Meta-policy pattern that activates when the platform is one of the batch-oriented platform types (batch, data-pipeline, worker, ml-training) and enforces that the batch_processing feature flag is explicitly set to true. These platform types are semantically batch workloads: they execute scheduled or event-driven jobs rather than responding to real-time requests. Failing to declare batch_processing: true on a batch platform means the compiler will not select batch infrastructure patterns (batch-processing-required, data-batch-processing--*) and will size the system as if it were a real-time API service — producing an architecturally incorrect output. This pattern closes that gap by making the implicit explicit: if your platform IS batch, you must declare it as such so the full batch enforcement chain fires.

cost.costNotes: Meta-policy pattern with no direct cost. Infrastructure costs are in downstream batch implementation patterns.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Zero cost — meta-policy pattern only. Batch compute costs are in the data-batch-processing--* variant patterns.
provenance.adoptionCost: Zero adoption cost — this pattern auto-activates on batch platform types and enforces batch_processing: true as a hard compilation requirement.
provenance.licenseCost: No license cost — policy enforcement pattern only.

provides.reasoning: Enforces that batch_processing: true is declared whenever the spec uses a batch-oriented platform type, preventing silent mis-classification of batch systems as real-time services.

availability.reason: General architectural enforcement pattern applicable to any batch platform regardless of technology or cloud provider

supports_nfr.reason: Batch platforms are compatible with any authentication mechanism; job-level auth is handled by IAM roles and scheduler credentials.
Batch job execution logs serve as an audit trail; compatible with both audited and non-audited environments.
Batch platforms support tenant isolation through job queue namespacing, separate worker pools, or IAM-scoped job execution.
Batch platform availability ceiling matches managed batch scheduler SLAs. Five-nine availability requires active-active multi-region batch topology.
Batch platforms are compatible with PII workloads when data at rest and in transit is encrypted and job access is IAM-controlled.
Compatible with GDPR when batch jobs support configurable data retention and deletion workflows.
Compatible with HIPAA when batch jobs enforce encryption at rest and in transit with appropriate BAA coverage.
Compatible with CCPA when batch jobs support consumer data deletion within required timeframes.
Compatible with SOX when batch job execution logs provide an immutable audit trail of data processing operations.
Batch platforms are inherently eventually consistent. If read-your-writes is required, compensating patterns must be added on top.
Durability depends on batch checkpointing strategy. With idempotent jobs and checkpointing, strict durability is achievable.

supports_constraints.reason: Primary activation gate — these platform types are semantically batch workloads. api, web, mobile, desktop, iot, library, cli platforms are excluded because they are real-time or interactive, not scheduled batch.
Batch platform enforcement is cloud-agnostic; managed batch services exist on all major clouds and on-premises.
Intentionally compatible with both true and false — this pattern must activate even when batch_processing is false or unset so it can enforce the correct value via requires_constraints.
Batch platforms work with or without caching.
Batch platforms can use async messaging for job queuing but do not require it.
Batch platforms are commonly used for batch AI inference (embedding generation, bulk predictions) but are not limited to AI workloads.
Batch platforms support both single-tenant and multi-tenant job architectures.
Batch platforms can coordinate distributed transactions within a job but do not require them.
Batch platforms and real-time streaming are complementary; lambda architecture combines both.
Batch platforms are used for offline vector index building and bulk embedding generation.
Batch platforms are compatible with document stores as the source or sink for batch jobs.
Batch platforms can use key-value stores for job state and deduplication.
Batch platforms support graph analytics and bulk graph mutation jobs.
Batch platforms are commonly used for time-series aggregation and historical rollup jobs.
Batch platforms can run bulk OLTP operations during off-peak windows.
Batch platforms naturally support OLAP via scheduled analytical queries and aggregation jobs.

requires_constraints.reason: Batch-oriented platforms (batch, data-pipeline, worker, ml-training) must explicitly declare batch_processing: true. Without this flag the compiler will not activate batch infrastructure patterns (batch-processing-required, data-batch-processing--*), leaving the spec without correctly sized batch scheduling, job start latency enforcement, or throughput validation.
